zotero.abstractNote: Bayesian Quadrature (BQ) is a powerful framework for probabilistic numerical integration. However, standard BQ methods rely on Euclidean assumptions, which are often violated when data lies on a low-dimensional manifold embedded in a high-dimensional space. We propose a BQ method that accounts for the geometry of the data manifold by using a Riemannian kernel. We derive a closed-form solution for the integral of the kernel, which allows for efficient computation of the BQ estimator. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several synthetic and real-world datasets, showing that it outperforms standard BQ methods in terms of accuracy and uncertainty quantification.
